did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper (Abstract 246P) utilized artificial intelligence to obtain results in dermatology. Specifically, it employed AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI) to provide malignancy scores and an AI-derived confirmation model (ConvNeXt) using line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) to confirm basal cell carcinoma (BCC) diagnoses in Gorlin syndrome patients. Additionally, the paper references other studies using deep learning pipelines for melanoma detection and machine learning models for predicting immune checkpoint inhibitor pneumonitis.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is labeled as 'abstracts' and specifically identified as '246P', indicating it is a conference abstract. The content describes an original observational study involving three Gorlin syndrome patients monitored with multimodal imaging (VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT) and AI to detect basal cell carcinomas. It reports original methods, results (100% sensitivity and specificity), and conclusions, which fits the criteria for a Research paper (conference abstract with original results).

title: Cutaneous follow-up of Gorlin syndrome patients with VECTRA 3D® total body photography and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography assisted by artificial intelligence for the diagnosis of difficult-to-detect basal cell carcinomas
brief_summary: This study evaluated a multimodal non-invasive imaging approach using VECTRA 3D total body photography and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) assisted by artificial intelligence for the diagnosis of difficult-to-detect basal cell carcinomas in three Gorlin syndrome patients. The protocol involved using AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI) to provide malignancy scores and an AI-derived confirmation model (ConvNeXt) via LC-OCT to confirm diagnoses prior to surgery. The evaluation focused on 17 difficult-to-diagnose basal cell carcinomas, including small lesions lacking specific dermoscopic criteria. The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity across all 17 lesions, thereby reducing unnecessary biopsies and facilitating one-step surgical management. The authors conclude that this AI-supported imaging strategy enables early and accurate diagnosis, improving patient follow-up and management for Gorlin syndrome.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focused on diagnosing difficult-to-detect basal cell carcinomas in three Gorlin syndrome patients. Specifically, 17 difficult-to-diagnose BCCs (small <2 mm lesions lacking specific dermoscopic criteria) were included in the evaluation. The lesions were described as pigmented and non-pigmented, superficial and nodular.

all_task_types.details: The study utilized an AI-derived confirmation model (ConvNeXt) via line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) to confirm BCC diagnoses. The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity across 17 difficult-to-diagnose BCC lesions, indicating a binary classification task (BCC vs. non-BCC or confirmation of BCC).

A deep learning pipeline performed real-time segmentation and risk prediction for melanoma. The text mentions 'risk prediction' and 'high-risk lesions', which typically implies classification into risk categories, though specific class labels are not detailed beyond 'high-risk'.

datasets_used.name: Gorlin Syndrome Patient Cohort (CHU de Marseille)
datasets_used.description: A private clinical cohort consisting of three Gorlin syndrome patients (one 40-year-old man, one 37-year-old woman, and one 72-year-old woman) monitored at the Dermatology / Automated Melanoma Screening Center, CHU de Marseille - Hôpital de la Timone, Marseille, France. The cohort included 17 difficult-to-diagnose basal cell carcinomas (BCCs), specifically small (<2 mm) lesions lacking specific dermoscopic criteria, which were pigmented and non-pigmented, superficial and nodular.

use.testing: The 17 lesions were used for the evaluation of the multimodal imaging approach (VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT with AI) to assess sensitivity and specificity.

datasets_used.name: Melanoma Detection Training Dataset
datasets_used.description: A dataset comprising >445,000 clinical and dermoscopic images, including histologically confirmed melanomas, used to train the deep learning pipeline for the low-cost 3D melanoma scanner described in Abstract 242P (separate study within the same publication context).

use.training: Used to train the deep learning pipeline for real-time segmentation and risk prediction of melanoma.

datasets_used.name: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor Pneumonitis (CIP) Prediction Cohorts
datasets_used.description: Data from 4 real-world retrospective cohort studies identified via a systematic review of Embase and PubMed databases, used to compare artificial intelligence-based prediction models for immune checkpoint inhibitor related pneumonitis (Abstract 247P).

use.validation: Used for calculating pooled area under the curve (AUC) metrics in the meta-analysis.
use.other: Used for systematic review and meta-analysis of model performance.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To compare the predictive capability of machine learning models for immune checkpoint inhibitor pneumonitis by calculating pooled area under the curve (AUC) metrics from validation cohorts.
To quantitatively identify strong models for future clinical application by aggregating performance metrics across 4 real-world retrospective cohort studies.
To estimate the uncertainty of the pooled AUC estimates for clinical input models, radiomic input models, and multimodal models.
To assess heterogeneity among the studies included in the meta-analysis.
To evaluate the performance of the multimodal non-invasive imaging approach (VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT with AI) in diagnosing basal cell carcinomas in Gorlin syndrome patients.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: No formal hypothesis testing (e.g., p-values, significance tests) was reported in the provided abstracts. The study reports descriptive performance metrics (100% sensitivity and specificity) and meta-analytic results (pooled AUCs with confidence intervals and I² statistics) without inferential hypothesis testing.

Gorlin Syndrome Patient Cohort (Abstract.dataset_description: Three Gorlin syndrome patients monitored with VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT; 17 difficult-to-diagnose basal cell carcinomas (BCCs) evaluated.
Gorlin Syndrome Patient Cohort (Abstract.notes: The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity across 17 lesions, reducing unnecessary biopsies and facilitating one-step surgical management. AI assistance supported diagnostic training for less experienced clinicians.

Melanoma Detection Training Dataset (Abs.dataset_description: Deep learning pipeline trained on >445,000 clinical and dermoscopic images (including histologically confirmed melanomas).
Melanoma Detection Training Dataset (Abs.notes: The prototype demonstrated high sensitivity and precision in detecting high-risk lesions on synthetic and real-world skin surface models during internal validation.

Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor Pneumonitis.dataset_description: 4 real-world retrospective cohort studies (N=3052) used for systematic review and meta-analysis of ML models for CIP prediction.
Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor Pneumonitis.notes: Clinical-radiomic models provided the best pre-ICI predictive performance for CIP, closely followed by radiomic-only models. Clinical-radiomic model results were restricted due to a small sample size from only 2 studies.

architecture_details.application: AI-derived confirmation model for Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC) diagnosis using line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT)

training_details.training_data_source: Not explicitly reported for ConvNeXt in Abstract 246P; however, a separate deep learning pipeline for melanoma detection (Abstract 242P) was trained on >445,000 clinical and dermoscopic images

classification_or_output_head.performance: 100% sensitivity and specificity across 17 difficult-to-diagnose BCC lesions

preprocessing_techniques: Posture-aware mapping for accurate lesion localization in 3D photogrammetry
Local image processing for GDPR compliance
conclusions: The multimodal non-invasive imaging approach using VECTRA 3D total body photography and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) assisted by artificial intelligence achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity in diagnosing 17 difficult-to-detect basal cell carcinomas in three Gorlin syndrome patients.
The AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI) provided malignancy scores, and the AI-derived confirmation model (ConvNeXt) using LC-OCT confirmed basal cell carcinoma diagnoses prior to surgery.
This AI-supported imaging strategy reduces unnecessary biopsies and facilitates one-step surgical management for basal cell carcinomas.
AI assistance with VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT supports diagnostic training for less experienced clinicians, although expert interpretation remains crucial.

The innovative multimodal non-invasive imaging with VECTRA 3D and LC-OCT, supported by AI, enables early and accurate diagnosis of basal cell carcinomas in Gorlin syndrome patients, drastically improving patient follow-up and management.
important_remarks: The primary study (Abstract 246P) is a case series with an extremely small sample size (N=3 patients, 17 lesions), which limits statistical power and generalizability.
The reported performance metrics of 100% sensitivity and specificity are likely optimistic due to the small, non-representative sample and lack of external validation.

The AI confirmation model (ConvNeXt) was applied to a single institutional cohort without reported training/validation splits or independent testing sets, raising concerns about overfitting or data leakage.
The study population is restricted to Gorlin syndrome patients with difficult-to-diagnose BCCs, limiting the external validity of the findings to the general dermatology population.
The paper is a conference abstract, providing limited methodological detail regarding the AI model training, hyperparameter tuning, and specific inclusion/exclusion criteria for the 17 lesions.
No p-values or confidence intervals were reported for the diagnostic accuracy metrics in the primary study, preventing assessment of statistical significance.
The study acknowledges that expert interpretation remains crucial, suggesting the AI tool is intended as an assistive device rather than a standalone diagnostic solution.
